headline: Boeing is hiring 100 to 140 factory workers a week to boost production
summary: Boeing is adding roughly 100–140 factory workers per week, its fastest pace since 2024, as it targets higher production and replaces retirees. The Boeing hiring push spans assembly and support roles and is tied to 737 MAX production increases and ongoing 777X work. Interest in Boeing jobs and apprenticeship programs has risen, with participation exceeding prior caps. Union leader Jon Holden said the Pacific Northwest’s union-represented workforce has surpassed 34,000, while Washington state aerospace employment rebounded from about 79,000 last August to 81,800 in February. The buildup is steadier than the 2023–2024 surge as aircraft deliveries trend toward their highest levels since 2018 and regulators allow gradual 737 MAX capacity increases.

headline: Why Boeing, GE Supplier Woodward Has Clear Skies Ahead
summary: Woodward's energy conversion and control systems help to power the Boeing 737 MAX, Joint Direct Attack Munitions and data centers — three of the engines key to fueling growth for the S&P 400 MidCap firm.  In addition to Boeing, customers accounting for at least 10% of sales for its aerospace or industrial segments in 2025 included RTX, GE Aerospace, GE Vernova, Caterpillar and Rolls-Royce.  Woodward is part of the flagship IBD 50 list of leading growth stocks.
